pluginName.description: Optional. Plugin name to display in the progress bar.

pluginPath.description:
Path to the plugin directory as absolute path
(/wordpress/wp-content/plugins/plugin-name); or the plugin entry file
relative to the plugins directory (plugin-name/plugin-name.php).

themeFolderName.description: The name of the theme folder inside wp-content/themes/

anyOf.description: The Blueprint declaration, typically stored in a blueprint.json file.

constants.description: PHP Constants to define on every request

description.description:
Optional description. It doesn't do anything but is exposed as
a courtesy to developers who may want to document which blueprint
file does what.

extraLibraries.description: Extra libraries to preload into the Playground instance.

networking.description: Should boot with support for network request via wp_safe_remote_get?

landingPage.description: The URL to navigate to after the blueprint has been run.

login.description:
User to log in as.
If true, logs the user in as admin/password.

meta.description: Optional metadata. Used by the Blueprints gallery at https://github.com/WordPress/blueprints

author.description: A GitHub username of the author of this Blueprint.

categories.description:
Relevant categories to help users find your Blueprint in the future
Blueprints section on WordPress.org.

description.description: A brief explanation of what your Blueprint offers.

title.description: A clear and concise name for your Blueprint.

plugins.description: WordPress plugins to install and activate

preferredVersions.description: The preferred PHP and WordPress versions to use.

php.description:
The preferred PHP version to use.
If not specified, the latest supported version will be used

wp.description:
The preferred WordPress version to use.
If not specified, the latest supported version will be used

steps.description:
The steps to run after every other operation in this Blueprint was
executed.

username.description: The user to log in as. Defaults to 'admin'.

options.description: The options to set on the site.

step.description: The name of the step. Must be "setSiteOptions".

Blueprint.description:
WordPress Playground Blueprint configuration.
Can be either an inline blueprint object or a file path to a blueprint JSON file.

DefineSiteUrlStep.description: Changes the site URL of the WordPress installation.defineSiteUrl

method.description:
The method of defining the constants in wp-config.php. Possible values are:

- rewrite-wp-config: Default. Rewrites the wp-config.php file to
                     explicitly call define() with the requested
                     name and value. This method alters the file
                     on the disk, but it doesn't conflict with
                     existing define() calls in wp-config.php.

- define-before-run: Defines the constant before running the requested
                     script. It doesn't alter any files on the disk, but
                     constants defined this way may conflict with existing
                     define() calls in wp-config.php.

resource.description: Identifies the file resource as a git directory

.git.description: When true, include a `.git` directory with Git metadata (experimental).

ref.description: The ref (branch, tag, or commit) of the git repository

refType.description: Explicit hint about the ref type (branch, tag, commit, refname)

url.description: The URL of the git repository

importer.description:
The importer to use. Possible values:

- `default`: The importer from https://github.com/humanmade/WordPress-Importer
- `data-liberation`: The experimental Data Liberation WXR importer developed at
                     https://github.com/WordPress/wordpress-playground/issues/1894

This option is deprecated. The syntax will not be removed, but once the
Data Liberation importer matures, it will become the only supported
importer and the `importer` option will be ignored.

Mount.description:
Filesystem mount configuration.
Maps local filesystem paths to WordPress Playground virtual filesystem.
Extends the base Mount type from

beforeInstall.description:
Whether to mount before WordPress installation.
When true, the mount happens before WordPress is installed.
When false or omitted, the mount happens after WordPress is fully installed and booted.

hostPath.description:
Absolute or relative path on the host filesystem to mount.
Relative paths are resolved from projectHostPath.

vfsPath.description: Virtual filesystem path where the host path should be mounted.

body.description:
Request body.
If an object is given, the request will be encoded as multipart
and sent with a `multipart/form-data` header.

$_SERVER.description: $_SERVER entries to set for this run.

code.description: The code snippet to eval instead of a php file.

env.description: Environment variables to set for this run.

relativeUri.description: Request path following the domain:port part.

scriptPath.description: Path of the .php file to execute.

PHPUnitConfig.description: PHPUnit configuration for running PHP unit tests.

bootstrapPath.description:
Path to PHPUnit bootstrap file (relative to project root)
If not provided, only the custom wp-tester bootstrap will be used

configPath.description: Path to PHPUnit configuration file (relative to project root)

phpunitArgs.description: Additional arguments to pass to PHPUnit

phpunitPath.description: Path to PHPUnit executable (relative to project root)

testMode.description: Test mode - determines whether WordPress is loaded during tests

Reporter.description:
Reporter configuration.
Can be a simple string for reporters without options,
or a tuple of [reporter name, options] for configurable reporters.

path.description: The path to remove
The path to remove

filename.description:
This property is ignored during Blueprint v1 execution but exists
so the same runPHP step structure can be used for Blueprints v1 and v2.

sql.description: The SQL to run. Each non-empty line must contain a valid SQL query.

Tests.description: Test configuration specifying which test suites to run.

phpunit.description:
PHPUnit test configuration.
When provided, runs PHPUnit tests with the specified paths.
When undefined, PHPUnit tests are disabled.

plugin.description:
Plugin slug to test.
When provided, runs plugin-specific tests including activation, deactivation, and load tests.

theme.description:
Theme slug to test.
When provided, runs theme-specific tests including activation and homepage load tests.

wp.description:
Whether to run WordPress core tests.
Tests WordPress boot, admin dashboard, and REST API.

filesTree.description:
The 'filesTree' defines the directory structure, supporting 'literal:directory' or
'git:directory' types. The 'name' represents the root directory, while 'files' is an object
where keys are file paths, and values contain either file content as a string or nested objects
for subdirectories.

writeToPath.description: The path of the file to write to

description:
Complete wp-tester configuration.
This is the root configuration object for wp-tester.json files.

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference for IDE validation and autocomplete

environments.description:
Test environments to run.
Each environment can have different PHP/WordPress versions and setup.
Tests will run against all defined environments (matrix testing).

projectHostPath.description:
Project root directory (host filesystem path).
All relative paths in the config are resolved from this directory.
If a relative path is provided, it is relative to the config file location.

projectType.description:
Detected WordPress project type.
Automatically detected during setup based on project structure.

projectVFSPath.description:
Path where your project directory will be mounted in the WordPress environment.

When not specified, the path is automatically determined based on projectType:
- plugin: /wordpress/wp-content/plugins/{dir-name}
- theme: /wordpress/wp-content/themes/{dir-name}
- wp-content: /wordpress/wp-content
- wordpress: /wordpress
- other: **Required** - You must specify where your project directory should be mounted
